Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your team can access inventory data anytime, anywhere, and keeps customer and supplier information secure and compliant with Singapore's PDPA rules. Your warehouse staff need mobile access to scan and update stock on the shop floor, while only authorised people should see sensitive pricing or supplier terms. Without this foundation, your data becomes a liability and your team wastes time hunting for information.

Inventory Core.narrative: This is the heartbeat of inventory management — a single source of truth for what you have, where it is, and what it costs. SMEs in Singapore often juggle multiple warehouses or storage locations, and manual tracking leads to stockouts, overstock, and write-offs. Real-time stock visibility, barcode scanning, and lot tracking help you fulfil orders faster, reduce shrinkage, and prove traceability to customers or regulators.

Procurement.narrative: This family streamlines how you order from suppliers and manage relationships — from raising a PO to tracking delivery and payment. For SMEs buying raw materials or finished goods regularly, a structured procurement process cuts lead times, prevents duplicate orders, and gives you visibility into supplier performance. When your supplier is late or quality slips, you'll know immediately and can escalate or switch vendors.

Warehouse & Logistics.narrative: This family orchestrates the physical movement of goods — receiving, storing, picking, packing, and shipping — with full traceability and minimal errors. SMEs that operate multiple locations, fulfil customer orders quickly, or handle returns need these capabilities to avoid picking errors, reduce labour costs, and meet customer delivery promises. Dropship and assembly features also let you serve customers without holding all inventory yourself.

Planning & Compliance.narrative: This family helps you avoid stockouts and excess inventory by automating reorder triggers based on demand patterns and safety stock rules. For SMEs with seasonal demand or long supplier lead times, smart replenishment means you order just enough, just in time — freeing up cash and warehouse space. It's the difference between guessing and planning.

Analytics & Intelligence.narrative: This family gives you dashboards and reports on inventory turns, order fulfilment speed, and supplier reliability — so you can spot trends and make faster decisions. SMEs often rely on gut feel; analytics let you see which products are slow movers, which suppliers are reliable, and where cash is tied up. One dashboard showing fill rate and lead time can reveal quick wins in your supply chain.
